key_evidence: HRW World Report 2026 (India chapter): Bangladesh reported 1,500+ men, women and children expelled May-June without due process; 'several of them turned out to be Indian citizens, mostly impoverished migrant workers'

HRW: at least 192 UNHCR-registered Rohingya refugees unlawfully expelled (refoulement); 40 Rohingya placed on a navy ship, beaten, given life jackets and tossed into the sea near Myanmar — UN special rapporteur cited 'blatant disregard for the lives and safety' of protected persons

Polis Project: government-directed regime — May 2 SOP eliminated the requirement to notify Bangladesh before deportation; Union Home Ministry ordered all states to set up Special Task Forces to deport alleged migrants within 30 days; Immigration and Foreigners Bill 2025 mandates detention centers in every state; no FIRs, no counsel, night detentions; Indian citizens (e.g. pregnant Sunali Khatun) deported and ordered returned by the Calcutta High Court
HRW: 10,000+ Gujarat structures demolished in violation of the Supreme Court's Nov 2024 ruling against arbitrary demolitions — direct executive defiance of the judiciary
HRW: 64 documented anti-Muslim hate-speech incidents within 10 days of the April Pahalgam attack; targeting of Kashmiri students, vendors and workers

Held at 15.6 (NOT the 0.0 floor) because functioning institutions demonstrably contest and reverse some acts (Calcutta HC return order), distinguishing India from the uncontested sole-perpetrator floor and from the multi-sided Burkina Faso case (6.3)
methodology_note: ACTIVE-STATE-PERPETRATION-REWEIGHT (ruling established 2026-05-31, Burkina Faso) applied to India. The published uniform-~1.9 baseline (22.7) predates and does not reflect HRW World Report 2026's corroborated (Tier-5) finding of a government-directed refoulement-and-expulsion regime targeting Bengali Muslims and Rohingya, including expulsion of Indian citizens and life-endangering sea-toss of refugees. Downgrade concentrated in EQU (1.8->1.3: deliberate religious targeting, refoulement, citizenship-stripping), EMP (1.9->1.4: beatings, sea endangerment, hate-speech surge), ACC (1.9->1.4: due-process collapse via May 2 SOP, no acknowledgment, no repair), INT (2.0->1.5: defiance of the Supreme Court demolition ruling). AWR/ACT/BND/SYS held marginally above baseline (residual functioning institutions and judicial check). Scored to 15.6, NOT to 0.0, because courts demonstrably reverse some acts — distinct from the absolute floor (Myanmar, Sudan) and above the multi-sided active-perpetration case (Burkina Faso, 6.3). BAND CROSSING (Developing 22.7 -> Critical 15.6); flagged for human review. Canonical reconstruction exact at 15.6 (integration premium 0.0).
